finding_results.notes: All refrigerator-click / forty-minute-interval motif instances removed. Line 29: replaced with hot-water pipes knocking as heating cycles ('Twice the hot-water pipes knocked in the wall beside her — the building's heating cycling on and off, the old brownstone running its own clock while she read'). Line 129: replaced 'the refrigerator's click' with 'the boiler's low thud from the basement' in the catalog of ambient apartment sounds. Line 173: replaced the primary midnight scene-anchor with a pipe contraction as the heating shuts down for the night, with an added observation tying Mira's involuntary attention to her having stopped planning — connecting the ambient sound to the chapter's thematic arc ('She heard it the way you hear things when you've stopped planning: completely and uselessly'). Line 175: updated 'clicking' to 'ticking' to match revised sound reference. The refrigerator remains at lines 9, 11, and 177 as a classified asset (energy profile, coefficient, infrastructure list), which serves the classification plot rather than the ambient-sound motif. No new recurring-pattern instances introduced — each replacement sound is distinct (pipes knocking, boiler thud, pipe contraction) to avoid substituting one motif for another.

Somatic response rerouted from chest to jaw: 'Her jaw locked — teeth clamped on the sound before it got out.' Routes through a body part consistent with Mira's stress-tell profile (under acute stress, her syntax clips and she goes quiet after fury). The locked jaw flows into the existing grip-the-sink beat and makes the pressurized thought ('Fuck your locally optimal') read as words trapped behind clenched teeth — the profanity arriving as interior pressure rather than vocalization. Line 161's sternum reference ('Something behind her sternum burned, hot and chemical') preserved unchanged as this chapter's single permitted chest-locus instance. Line 147's 'something opened in her chest' (hope, not somatic stress) is a distinct usage at the sustained-objection pivot and does not duplicate the stress-tell pattern flagged in F03.

summary: Two MEDIUM findings addressed with five targeted lexical edits across four locations. F01: all refrigerator-click / forty-minute-interval motif instances (lines 29, 129, 173, 175) replaced with distinct ambient domestic sounds rooted in the brownstone's heating infrastructure — hot-water pipes knocking (line 29), boiler thud (line 129), pipe contraction as heating shuts down at midnight (line 173), and consistent 'ticking' reference (line 175). Each replacement is specific to its scene's hour and Mira's attentional state; no single replacement recurs enough to constitute a new motif. F03: chest somatic response at line 105 rerouted to jaw, consistent with Mira's stress-tell profile; the stronger sternum-burn at line 161 preserved as the chapter's single chest-locus instance. No structural changes, no new recurring patterns introduced, heading contract preserved, continuity with adjacent chapters maintained.
